reasoning: In mid-April 2025, Lesotho officially granted Starlink a 10-year operating license, with several news outlets reporting that the country "speedran" the approval process. Furthermore, a May 6, 2025 article explicitly reported that Starlink had established its presence in Lesotho in April 2025 (incorporating it into its 24 active African markets alongside other recent additions like Congo, Somalia, and Guinea-Bissau). Because Starlink already has established service in nearby countries (like Eswatini, Botswana, and Zimbabwe), the actual satellite and routing infrastructure is ready to serve the footprint. Capacity in the region is generally abundant, meaning the country will launch as "Available" rather than "Waitlist." While the map natively read "2025" directly following the mid-April license announcements, there is a very high probability that within the 2.5 months between the license grant and the July 1 deadline, the Starlink Availability Map will be updated to show Lesotho as "Available."
